titleStmt.title: Letter from J.T. Burns to George Gavan Duffy, 16 October 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: J.T. Burns

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter from J.T. Burns to George Gavan Duffy (1882-1951) refers to a telephone conversation. The writer encloses a copy of the Royal Warrant for Maintenance of Discipline among Prisoners of War. The document has not survived.George Gavan Duffy, was an Irish politician, barrister and judge. He unsuccessfully defended Roger Casement at his trial for high treason after the Easter Rising.J.T. Burns was a Lieutenant and Adjutant at the Frongoch Prisoner of War Camp, Wales.

msIdentifier.collection: Bureau of Military History Contemporary Documents, George Gavan Duffy Collection BMH CD/45/6/3

correspAction.placeName: Prisoner of War Camp, Frongoch, near Bala, Wales.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
George Gavan Duffy, Esq., White Lion hotel Bala With reference to your telephonic communication of
this morning, i forward herewith a copy of royal Warrant
for Maintenance of Discipline among Prisoners of War. J.T. Burns
Lieut & Adjt.
for Colonel,
Commandant Prisoners of War Camp, Frongoch. nr. Bala 16th Octr. 1916
